description: Add proactive_nudge message type to the WebSocket push path on the backend and handle it on the frontend chat WebSocket, then reduce polling interval to 10 minutes as a fallback.
steps: Step 1 - Backend: In backend/app/scheduler/proactive_delivery.py, change the WebSocket push message type from 'notification' to 'proactive_nudge'. Update the payload structure to match the required format: {type: 'proactive_nudge', content: 'nudge message text', notification_id: '...', tier: N, action: {...}}. The delivery function already has push=True support with manager.send_to_user() - just change the message format sent.

Step 2 - Frontend ws.ts: In frontend/lib/ws.ts, add 'proactive_nudge' to the ResponsePayload type union. The type field should become: 'text' | 'audio' | 'task_update' | 'status' | 'error' | 'text_chunk' | 'text_end' | 'proactive_nudge'. Also add optional fields: notification_id?: string, tier?: number, action?: { label: string; kind: string } | null.

Step 3 - Frontend useChatSocket.ts: In frontend/hooks/useChatSocket.ts, add handling for the 'proactive_nudge' message type in the ws.on('message') handler. It should NOT add the nudge as a chat message. Instead, dispatch a CustomEvent 'chronai-proactive-nudge' on the window with the nudge data as detail, so the ProactiveListener can pick it up. This keeps separation of concerns clean - the chat hook handles receiving, the ProactiveListener handles displaying.

Step 4 - Frontend ProactiveListener.tsx: In frontend/components/notifications/ProactiveListener.tsx, add a useEffect that listens for the 'chronai-proactive-nudge' window event. When received, call addNotification with the nudge data (same format currently used for polled interventions). Also update shownRef to track it. Dispatch 'chronai-notifications-changed' event to refresh the bell.

Step 5 - Frontend ProactiveListener.tsx: Reduce CHECK_INTERVAL_MS from 3 * 60_000 (3 minutes) to 10 * 60_000 (10 minutes) since real-time pushes now handle the immediate case. Keep the polling as a fallback for when WebSocket is disconnected.

Step 6 - Frontend useNotificationSocket.ts: Update the useNotificationSocket hook to also handle 'proactive_nudge' type messages (in addition to the existing 'notification' type), since the backend now sends 'proactive_nudge' type. This ensures nudges arrive even through the separate notification socket path.
acceptance_criteria: Backend proactive_delivery.py sends messages with type 'proactive_nudge' when push=True
useChatSocket.ts dispatches a window event when a proactive_nudge message arrives (does not add it as a chat message)
ProactiveListener.tsx listens for proactive_nudge window events and shows them as notifications via addNotification
ProactiveListener.tsx polling interval is 10 minutes instead of 3 minutes
Existing notification handling (type 'notification') still works in useNotificationSocket.ts
Focus/Pomodoro suppression still works (backend governance unchanged)
verification: Run `cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/frontend && pnpm build` and confirm it succeeds
Run `cd /projects/sandbox/ChronAI/frontend && pnpm lint` and confirm it passes
findings: All changes compile cleanly. pnpm build and pnpm lint both pass with zero errors. The proactive_nudge message type is now used on both push paths (chat WebSocket and notification WebSocket). Polling reduced to 10 minutes as a fallback. Existing notification type handling preserved in useNotificationSocket.ts. Post-review fixes applied: (1) removed proactive_nudge handling from useNotificationSocket to prevent duplicate notifications, (2) added title field to push payload, (3) added focus-state check in handleNudge as defense-in-depth, (4) added random suffix to fallback dedup key to prevent millisecond collisions.
